dimensions.label: Problem Clarity
dimensions.evidence: Concrete: one human reviewing ~12 parallel agents, reading cold drive-by diffs, across fragmented watch/review modes, behind a narrative pipeline too slow to use in a loop.

dimensions.label: Goal Definition
dimensions.evidence: North star locked ('nothing forgotten, decided fast') plus diff-first/instant, streamlined-streaming guide, one-surface, push-right auto-clear, and cross-repo scale as named goals; goals 5-6 are delivered in phases 3-4 under the full (no-tier-cut) build.

dimensions.label: Success Criteria
dimensions.evidence: Each phase has a pass/fail criterion; mechanical ones carry runnable check commands (vitest suites against existing test precedent, daemon-survives-terminal, skeptic non-author provider, auto-clear gate logic); the one genuinely subjective criterion (prose density) is tagged a judgment call.

dimensions.label: Scope Boundaries
dimensions.evidence: Five phases in scope with no tier cut (full scope = all five); explicit exclusions: multi-machine sync, human-reviewer routing, non-Claude authors.

dimensions.label: Consistency
dimensions.evidence: No conflicting requirements; agent-as-actor preserved (auto-merge deferred to human/policy in P4, delivered over P2's decision channel); skeptic uses --with codex (local GPT-class) or a config API provider — not --with-to-OpenAI-API, which is impossible.

problem: Nick runs ~12 coding agents in parallel across repositories and is the review bottleneck. He babysits a dozen tmux panes, forgets to return to finished work, and every change he does review he reads cold as a drive-by. Work sits unreviewed because nothing pulls him back to it, and there is no single place to see what every agent is doing or what is waiting on him.

Diff Dad today has two disconnected modes — `dad watch` (local working tree, in-flight on this branch) and `dad review` (GitHub PR) — and running them in different places is itself friction. Its core 'PRs into narrated stories' pipeline is too slow for a review loop: PR mode blocks on a full multi-chapter LLM generation (often via the local `claude -p` path, which dad's own loading screen warns is ~minutes and ~5-10x slower than the API) before showing anything, and the resulting prose isn't more revealing than a plain diff. The storytelling concept is valued; its latency and ceremony are not.

The fix is to make Diff Dad a single, daemon-backed command center that shows all in-flight work and everything awaiting review across repos, renders diffs instantly, and uses a streamlined, streaming guided walkthrough to build genuine understanding of each change — yours and the agents' — rather than narrate at you. This continues and broadens the prior agent-comment-loop ideation (whose watch mode is the foundation it builds on).
goals: One cross-repo home lists every unit — agent-running, your working trees, open PRs, and units awaiting review — so nothing depends on remembering to visit a pane.
Opening any unit renders the diff on first paint, with no blocking 'generating' screen; the guided walkthrough streams in over it.
The walkthrough is streamlined and streams per section (beat rail + interleaved prose + resolve strips), replacing cold diff-reading with guided understanding.
Watch and review stop being separate modes: local working trees, agent units, and PRs are one list distinguished by status, not by command.
(Phase 3) The review verdict is produced by a model in a different family than the author, giving an independent second opinion.
(Phase 4) Safe units auto-clear without the human (agent opens a PR); only non-clearing units enter the needs-you queue.

successCriteria.criterion: submit_for_review registers a unit that appears in the command center, with the agent able to receive the decision back over MCP
successCriteria.check: cd packages/cli && npx vitest run src/__tests__/review-units.test.ts — drives a full in-process MCP round-trip (enqueue + decision-back) against the store, following the mcp-tools.test.ts precedent; exits 0

successCriteria.criterion: Opening a unit shows the diff before the guide finishes — no blocking generate screen on the unit path
successCriteria.check: cd packages/web && npx vitest run src/state/__tests__/unit-gate.test.ts — asserts the unit data path exposes diff/files with the narrative still pending and never trips the blocking-generate gate (pure store/logic test, no DOM); exits 0

successCriteria.criterion: The guided walkthrough builds as beat rail + interleaved streamlined prose + resolve strips, streaming per section
successCriteria.check: cd packages/web && npx vitest run src/lib/__tests__/walkthrough-model.test.ts — asserts the walkthrough builder yields ordered beats (rail entries) + interleaved prose/diff sections + resolve items from a sample diff+guide (pure function, no DOM); exits 0

successCriteria.criterion: The daemon survives the launching terminal closing and serves a cross-repo queue
successCriteria.check: launchctl load the daemon; close the launching terminal; curl -s localhost:<port>/api/units returns units submitted from two different repos

successCriteria.criterion: The skeptic's verdict is produced by a model family different from the author's
successCriteria.check: cd packages/cli && npx vitest run src/__tests__/skeptic.test.ts — asserts recorded skeptic provider (engine returns result.provider) != author provider; exits 0

successCriteria.criterion: Auto-clear fires only when verdict is safe AND checks are green AND zero items to resolve; everything else queues
successCriteria.check: cd packages/cli && npx vitest run src/__tests__/auto-clear-gate.test.ts — exits 0

successCriteria.criterion: The notifier surfaces the pending needs-you count (tmux status line) matching the queue length
successCriteria.check: run the status script against a seeded queue of N; stdout count equals N

successCriteria.criterion: The guided walkthrough's prose feels scannable, not a wall (subjective; the no-wait/instant-diff half is covered mechanically by the diff-before-guide criterion above)

mvp.item: Diff-first guided review experience — beat rail (evolve ChapterTOC) + streamlined interleaved prose (evolve Chapter) + inline resolve strips; diff renders instantly, guide streams per section; API path becomes the default
mvp.reason: Delivers 'decided fast' and fixes the latency + ceremony that make today's narrative unusable; defines what a reviewable unit looks like

mvp.item: Daemon command center — cross-repo home (needs-you / in-flight / cleared), submit_for_review enqueues, tracks agents + working trees + PRs as one status-grouped list; persistent list first, launchd terminal-survival as the hardening slice
mvp.reason: Delivers the 'nothing forgotten' north star and unifies the watch/review modes that feel strange today

full.item: Cross-model skeptic — independent reviewer on a non-author model family (--with codex by default, or a config API provider); emits verdict + cited, confidence-scored concerns that feed the rail flags, resolve strips, and recommended action
full.reason: Turns the guide's flags into a verdict trustworthy enough to drive the recommended action and, later, auto-clear

full.item: Checks runner + auto-clear gate — run configured/detected build/test/lint/type commands in the worktree; auto-clear safe+green+0-to-resolve units (decision delivered over the daemon's MCP channel; the agent opens the PR; merge left to human/policy)
full.reason: Objective signal lets safe work skip the human (push-right) without trusting an LLM verdict alone

full.item: Notifier + stdio MCP proxy — tmux status count, macOS push (deep-link), stale escalation, periodic digest; global one-time MCP registration via a net-new stdio proxy routing to the daemon
full.reason: Pulls Nick back proactively and removes per-repo MCP re-registration at the 12-agent scale

stretch.item: Per-repo auto-merge opt-in (widen the auto-clear terminal action from open-PR to merge as trust grows)
On-demand 'Ask dad' Q&A inside the walkthrough (interrogate the diff conversationally)

outOfScope.reason: Per-machine daemon only; cross-machine sync is a future sibling workflow

outOfScope.item: Human-reviewer routing/assignment beyond opening a PR
outOfScope.reason: The brief makes the ask cheap; explicit reviewer selection is a separate workflow
Author assumed = Claude; the cross-model skeptic provides the independence

future: Widen auto-merge as empirical trust in the gate grows
Human-reviewer routing as a sibling workflow

phases.title: Review experience (diff-first guided walkthrough)
phases.notes: B+C: beat rail (evolve ChapterTOC.tsx) + streamlined interleaved prose (evolve Chapter.tsx) + inline resolve strips; reuse useScrollTracker for rail tracking. Diff-first non-blocking render (adopt watch mode's pattern in useNarrative for the unit path); per-section streaming. Deliberately switch the default AI path from local claude -p to the API for latency (a user-facing default change). Tests target pure builders/store logic — no DOM test infra exists today, so installing jsdom + @testing-library is a P1 setup step if render-level tests are wanted. Begins unifying watch/review toward one surface.

phases.title: Command center (daemon)
phases.notes: Cross-repo review-unit store (lift the agent-comments/store.ts pattern, key by repo+unit) + submit_for_review MCP tool + a decision-delivery channel back to the agent; needs-you / in-flight / cleared dashboard, status-grouped; tracks agent units, working trees, and PRs as one list. Build the persistent cross-repo list first (generalize server.ts), then launchd terminal-survival as the higher-risk hardening slice. Displays the review pipeline's output, so it follows P1.

phases.title: Cross-model skeptic
phases.notes: Independent reviewer on a non-author model family. Primary path: --with codex (local Codex CLI, GPT-class, no API key, reuses existing setup); alternative: a configured OpenAI API provider/model. Note: --with resolves ONLY to local CLIs (claude/codex/pi) per ai-runtime.ts, so it CANNOT select the OpenAI API — reaching that needs a config provider override, not --with. Emits verdict + cited, confidence-scored concerns that replace the same-model triage pass as the source of rail flags + recommended action.

phases.title: Checks + auto-clear gate
phases.notes: Run configured/detected build/test/lint/type commands in the worktree; populate the checks row; auto-clear when verdict safe + checks green + 0 to-resolve. Terminal action: the auto-clear/approve decision is delivered to the still-connected agent over P2's decision-delivery channel, and the agent's own tooling opens the PR with the brief as description — dad never touches git. Merge left to human/policy (auto-merge is a stretch opt-in). Per-repo config gates aggressiveness. Depends on P2's decision channel.

phases.title: Notifier + stdio proxy
phases.notes: tmux status-line pending count, macOS push (deep-link to a unit), stale escalation, periodic digest. Global one-time MCP registration via a net-new stdio proxy (dad mcp) routing to the daemon — today's MCP is HTTP-only (webStandardStreamableHttp at /mcp), so the stdio transport is from-scratch, not an adaptation. Replaces per-session registration.
